title: Podman machine start stuck at `Waiting for VM ...`
body:
### Issue Description

This is Podman running in MacOS ARM64

```
$podman version
```
```
$podman info
```
```
$ podman version
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 127.0.0.1:49555->127.0.0.1:50507: read: connection reset by peer
```

```
$ podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: qemu
version: 4.8.2

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 127.0.0.1:49556->127.0.0.1:50507: read: connection reset by peer
```
I followed through [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md). Tried removing, re-installing, Podman desktop, unsetting `SSH_AUTH_SOCK`, checking `ssh-add -L` etc. Even firewall is disabled for the Mac as per suggestion.

I am suspecting it's something related to SSH but can't figure out what is the issue here.

Any suggested are welcome. Looking for location where I can check logs or run Podman in verbose mode will be helpful.

```
$ podman --log-level=debug ps
```
```
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level=debug ps)
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "<$HOME>/.ssh/podman-machine-default" SHA256:<key> ssh-ed25519
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 127.0.0.1:49561->127.0.0.1:50507: read: connection reset by peer
DEBU[0003] Shutting down engines
```

On Podman desktop I see:
```
 [compose] Error getting compose version: Error: Failed to execute command: spawn <$HOME>/.local/share/containers/podman-desktop/extensions-storage/podman-desktop.compose/bin/docker-compose ENOENT
```

Podman installed with `brew install podman`. Podman desktop tried with direct as well with brew `brew install --cask podman-desktop`. I am able to run docker fine and also able to start docker image in Podman.

```
```
$ uname -a
Darwin <machine-name>.local 23.2.0 Darwin Kernel Version 23.2.0: Wed Nov 15 21:55:06 PST 2023; root:xnu-10002.61.3~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6020 arm64
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. brew install podman
2. podman machine init
3. podman machine start


### Describe the results you received

System stuck at `Starting VM...`

### Describe the results you expected

VM started and running as expected.

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: qemu
version: 4.8.2

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 127.0.0.1:49556->127.0.0.1:50507: read: connection reset by peer
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

$ uname -a
Darwin <machinename>.local 23.2.0 Darwin Kernel Version 23.2.0: Wed Nov 15 21:55:06 PST 2023; root:xnu-10002.61.3~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6020 arm64


### Additional information

```
$ echo $SSH_AUTH_SOCK
/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.Yw5BA959Qf/Listeners
```

comments.body:
I thought that was my bad. Have the same issue.

Adding to this: I tried initializing the machine with stable branch of coreos, same issue.

Have the same issue too. I have two podman machines in my macOS (arm M1). The first one:`podman-machine-default`, the second one: `usbTest`. They use the `fedora-coreos-39.20231119.2.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2` image, and they start / ssh / stop work well always. Later, seems I `brew upgrade` my qemu two or three times recent months. Now, i have tired below steps. 1. `podman machine rm` remove the first machine and its related files. 2. `podman machine init` downloads the latest fedora coreos image, it works. 3. `podman start --log-level debug` fails as this new machine hangs with below screenshot: 4. 5. As my second machine `usbTest` works, so i copy its image and rename `testSecond.qcow2` 6. `podman machine init --image-path=testSecond.qcow2 --log-level debug`, it works. 7. `podman start --log-level debug` fails as the same issue. In my eye, step 7 should work well with early image, but it is not. Not clear qemu or image issue.

I encountered the issue on Mac after the recent package updates via homebrew. The issue appears to be with `qemu`. I downgraded it to version `8.1.3`, and now podman machine starts successfully.

To get verbose output I used `podman --log-level=trace machine start`, and could see the VM freeze at some point during startup sequence.

@krvpal  Thanks. Looks like issue is with `qemu` as you said. I have other machine and it works fine. I checked version and it's with `8.1.3`. I will try manually installing `8.1.3` and then try again. I close after confirming and create issue with `qemu` instead.

Having the same issue after brew upgrade

podman: 4.8.2
qemu: 8.2.0
MacOS: Sonoma 14.2.1 (23C71) - the latest one

I was able to fix this by copying the `/opt/homebrew/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd` file from QEMU 8.1.3 into QEMU 8.2.0.
It looks like an issue with the UEFI firmware: https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/1990

Thanks @zlogic. This worked for me. I just downloaded it directly from [gitlab](https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/blob/86305e864191123dcf87c3af639fddfc59352ac6/pc-bios/edk2-aarch64-code.fd.bz2).

There is also workaround posted in issue by [Vincent Murphy](https://gitlab.com/vdm1) :https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/1990#note_1707002525 (I haven't tried it though). Copying here for reference:
```
$ brew --version
Homebrew 4.2.0-68-g7817518
Homebrew/homebrew-core (git revision bad6b2c8726; last commit 2023-12-25)
Homebrew/homebrew-cask (git revision 5a0d345709; last commit 2023-12-25)
$ /opt/homebrew/bin/qemu-system-aarch64 -version
QEMU emulator version 8.2.0
$ curl -LO https://kojipkgs.fedoraproject.org//packages/edk2/20231122/14.fc39/noarch/edk2-aarch64-20231122-14.fc39.noarch.rpm
$ brew install rpm2cpio
$ rpm2cpio <edk2-aarch64-20231122-14.fc39.noarch.rpm | cpio -idmv
$ jq -r '.. | .filename? | select(.)' usr/share/qemu/firmware/52-edk2-aarch64-verbose-qcow2.json
/usr/share/edk2/aarch64/QEMU_EFI-pflash.qcow2
/usr/share/edk2/aarch64/vars-template-pflash.qcow2
$ /opt/homebrew/bin/qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt,accel=hvf -cpu host -m $((1 * 1024)) -nographic -snapshot \
-pflash $PWD/usr/share/edk2/aarch64/QEMU_EFI-pflash.qcow2 \
Fedora-Cloud-Base-39-1.5.aarch64.qcow2
```

Closing issue as it's resolved for now. There is patch coming with `qemu@8.2.1` in next year ;) Jan 2024.

Hit this myself, wanted to add an alternative homebrew-specific workaround for this until the qemu fix lands:

1. `brew uninstall podman qemu`
2. Grab a copy of the previous homebrew formula for qemu working version v8.1.3: `wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/676c6922d79d24cc0794dd22250e3ea1167f2cd9/Formula/q/qemu.rb` - NB: i saved this to my ~/Downloads/ not to my /opt/homebrew install, there's no messing with homebrew's local formula git repo
3. Run `brew install qemu.rb` - there are some warnings but you'll see they're nothing to bother about in this case
4. Prevent homebrew upgrading this for now: `brew pin qemu` NB: you'll need to remember to unpin in january!
5. Then you can `brew install --ignore-dependencies podman ; podman machine init ; podman machine start`

> Hit this myself, wanted to add an alternative homebrew-specific workaround for this until the qemu fix lands:

Thanks! I tried every single fix I can find on SO sites, but nothing works. I almost already want to give up, then decided to take a quick search here.

I can confirm that this non-homebrew-specific fix works for me. thx!!!

If you force remove the desktop app's podman and qemu dependency manually with:
```
brew uninstall --ignore-dependencies qemu podman
```
then the desktop app takes care of qemu and podman and everything works as expected and you can use the brew installed desktop app.
I've already reported this issue for the cask:
https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-cask/issues/163116

> then the desktop app takes care of qemu and podman and everything works as expected

Hmm... I'm still getting

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/2840074/6e49992e-e8bd-4aa9-828b-962ff0d63811)

Here is my work log:

```sh
$ podman
-bash: podman: command not found

$ brew uninstall --ignore-dependencies qemu podman
Error: No such keg: /opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu

$ brew uninstall --ignore-dependencies podman
Error: No such keg: /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman

$ brew install podman-desktop
. . .
==> Pouring qemu--8.2.0.arm64_sonoma.bottle.tar.gz
==> Pouring podman--4.8.3.arm64_sonoma.bottle.tar.gz
🍺  podman-desktop was successfully installed!

$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
^C

$ podman machine start --log-level=debug
Waiting for VM ...
# and then get the above screenshot

$ brew info podman-desktop
==> podman-desktop: 1.6.4

$ brew info qemu
==> qemu: stable 8.2.0 (bottled), HEAD

$ uname -rms
Darwin 23.2.0 arm64

$ sw_vers
ProductName:		macOS
ProductVersion:		14.2.1
BuildVersion:		23C71
```

If I do `brew uninstall --ignore-dependencies qemu podman` now, then, the desktop app immediately reports that the podman package is missing. If I close it, then I won't even be able to start it again.

@AntonioSun You seem to missunderstand a bit what I meant. Install the cask:
```
brew install podman-desktop
```
Then remove the dependencies:
```
brew uninstall --ignore-dependencies --zap qemu podman
```
Then on the UI if you start up the desktop app it will report that podman is not installed. Click setup/install and that will start the podman installer. Click through the installer's steps and voila you are setup to run the `podman machine init` command.
Thanks @lakkeger, that worked for me on a M3.

I work around this issue by installing the head version of `qemu` on Jan 23, 2024. Following is the list of commands I ran.

```shell
# Install the head version of qemu from git repository.
brew unlink qemu
brew install --head qemu

# Recreate the machine.
podman machine rm
podman machine init

# Start the new machine.
podman machine start
```

After that, everything works as expected.

Thanks @lilac! `brew install --head qemu` did the trick for me. However I needed to first install qemu and then podman without the qemu dependency, just like @craigjperry2 suggested [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21088#issuecomment-1871502921). 

This worked for me (don't forget to unpin later):

```
brew install --head qemu
brew pin qemu
brew install --ignore-dependencies podman
brew install minikube
podman machine init --cpus 8 --memory 32768 --rootful
podman machine start
```

I had the same issue on Mac M1 Pro. "Waiting for VM".  Got it working by reverting to qemu 8.1.3. It started using latest podman 4.9.0.
- `podman machine init -m 3072` - tried it as suggested, did not help.
- I tried several earlier podman versions up to 4.8.0. No luck.
- `podman machine start --log-level=debug` - Retrieve more info about the failure. Thanks to this command I got out this error message from qemu: `Synchronous Exception at 0x00000000BC562000 ASSERT [ArmCpuDxe] /home/kraxel/projects/qemu/roms/edk2/ArmPkg/Library/DefaultExceptionHandlerLib/Arch64/DefaultExceptionHandler.c(343): ((BOOLEAN)(0==1))`
- Following hints from here: Some people suggest reverting to an older qemu - [qemu ASSERT [ArmCpuDxe] DefaultExceptionHandler.c:333 on Mac M3](https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/issues/1990), [Synchronous Exception at 0x00000000BC562000](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/21122), [podman machine start stuck "Waiting for VM" indefinitely with qemu synchronous exception MacOS M3](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20776#top)
- I managed to get it running by reverting qemu to 8.1.3 [qemu.rb](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/676c6922d79d24cc0794dd22250e3ea1167f2cd9/Formula/q/qemu.rb). Reverting brew version instructions here: [How To Install Older Versions of Homebrew Packages](https://nelson.cloud/how-to-install-older-versions-of-homebrew-packages/) 


**Edit:**
Looks like the answer is buried in many answers. A plea to the original author @tekawade. Please append the description to let people know that there's are 2 ways to fix: via the rollback of qemu or via applehv provider. I wasted 3 hours looking for the answer. Had it been in the first post it would have been only 30 mins lost. Cheers!
I have the same problem with qemu @8.2.1
